quiz.title: Accounting and Month-End Quiz
quiz.description: Test your understanding of accounting basics, month end and Mayday product workflows.

questions.question: When finance people talk about debits and credits, what are they mainly referring to?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Money coming in and money going out of the bank

answers.label: B
answers.text: The two sides of an accounting entry

answers.label: C
answers.text: Profit and loss categories

answers.label: D
answers.text: Customer and supplier balances

questions.question: What is the main rule of double-entry bookkeeping?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Every transaction must be recorded once

answers.label: B
answers.text: Every transaction must increase profit

answers.label: C
answers.text: Total debits must equal total credits

answers.label: D
answers.text: Every transaction must involve cash

questions.question: What does the P&L show?

answers.label: A
answers.text: What the business owns and owes on one date

answers.label: B
answers.text: Actual cash received and paid

answers.label: C
answers.text: Income, costs and profit over a period

answers.label: D
answers.text: The company's legal ownership structure

questions.question: What does a profit margin show?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Profit as a percentage of revenue

answers.label: B
answers.text: The total cash in the bank

answers.label: C
answers.text: The company's assets minus liabilities

answers.label: D
answers.text: The amount owed to suppliers

questions.question: What does the balance sheet show?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The company's income and costs for the month

answers.label: B
answers.text: What a company owns, owes and what belongs to owners at a point in time

answers.label: C
answers.text: Only the company's bank transactions

answers.label: D
answers.text: The company's future sales forecast

questions.question: Why is a cash flow statement different from the P&L?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The P&L shows cash only, while cash flow shows profit

answers.label: B
answers.text: The P&L shows income and costs by accounting period, while cash flow shows actual cash movements

answers.label: C
answers.text: They are two names for the same report

answers.label: D
answers.text: The cash flow statement only shows tax payments

questions.question: What is the difference between solvency and liquidity?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Solvency is about long-term financial health; liquidity is about short-term cash availability

answers.label: B
answers.text: Solvency is about sales; liquidity is about profit

answers.label: C
answers.text: Solvency is about tax; liquidity is about payroll

answers.label: D
answers.text: Solvency and liquidity mean exactly the same thing

questions.question: What does the accruals principle say?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Income and costs should only be recorded when cash moves

answers.label: B
answers.text: Income and costs should be recorded in the period they relate to

answers.label: C
answers.text: All costs should be recorded at year end only

answers.label: D
answers.text: Revenue should always be recorded when a customer pays

questions.question: Which situation is an example of an accrual?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Paying upfront for a 12-month software subscription

answers.label: B
answers.text: Receiving customer payment before delivering the service

answers.label: C
answers.text: Receiving legal work in June but getting the invoice in July

answers.label: D
answers.text: Buying equipment and using it for five years

questions.question: Which situation is an example of a prepayment?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Paying annual insurance upfront and spreading the cost over 12 months

answers.label: B
answers.text: Completing work before sending an invoice

answers.label: C
answers.text: Owing a supplier for work already completed

answers.label: D
answers.text: Paying interest on a bank loan

questions.question: What is the best distinction between bookkeeping and accounting?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Bookkeeping records transactions; accounting interprets, adjusts and reports the numbers

answers.label: B
answers.text: Bookkeeping is only for large businesses; accounting is only for small businesses

answers.label: C
answers.text: Bookkeeping prepares tax returns; accounting only processes invoices

answers.label: D
answers.text: There is no difference

questions.question: What is month end?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The process of closing down a company

answers.label: B
answers.text: The recurring process of finalising the accounts for a month

answers.label: C
answers.text: The date when all invoices must legally be paid

answers.label: D
answers.text: The annual process of filing statutory accounts

questions.question: What is year end?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The process of closing the accounts for the financial year

answers.label: B
answers.text: The monthly bank reconciliation process

answers.label: C
answers.text: The process of paying employees

answers.label: D
answers.text: The date a customer contract starts

questions.question: What is a journal in accounting?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A customer-facing invoice

answers.label: B
answers.text: An accounting entry posted directly into the ledger

answers.label: C
answers.text: A bank statement

answers.label: D
answers.text: A shareholder report

questions.question: What is depreciation?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Recording a customer payment

answers.label: B
answers.text: Spreading the cost of a fixed asset over its useful life

answers.label: C
answers.text: Charging VAT on a sale

answers.label: D
answers.text: Paying a dividend to shareholders

questions.question: What is an invoice?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A document requesting payment for goods or services

answers.label: B
answers.text: A list of shareholders

answers.label: C
answers.text: A monthly profit report

answers.label: D
answers.text: A bank reconciliation

questions.question: What is an audit?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A sales forecast

answers.label: B
answers.text: An independent review of a company's financial statements and supporting records

answers.label: C
answers.text: A customer payment request

answers.label: D
answers.text: A process for setting marketing budgets

questions.question: What are management accounts used for?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Internal reporting and decision-making

answers.label: B
answers.text: Filing a company's official annual accounts only

answers.label: C
answers.text: Replacing all bookkeeping

answers.label: D
answers.text: Paying VAT automatically

questions.question: What are statutory accounts?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Informal weekly finance updates

answers.label: B
answers.text: Official financial statements prepared to meet legal and regulatory requirements

answers.label: C
answers.text: Sales dashboards for marketing teams

answers.label: D
answers.text: Internal-only management reports

questions.question: What is the main difference between management accounts and statutory accounts?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Management accounts are external; statutory accounts are internal

answers.label: B
answers.text: Management accounts help run the business; statutory accounts meet formal reporting requirements

answers.label: C
answers.text: Management accounts only show tax; statutory accounts only show sales

answers.label: D
answers.text: They are the same thing

questions.question: What does EBITDA stand for?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Earnings Before Income, Tax, Debt and Assets

answers.label: B
answers.text: Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation

answers.label: C
answers.text: Equity Before Interest, Tax, Dividends and Assets

answers.label: D
answers.text: Estimated Business Income, Tax, Debt and Amortisation

questions.question: Which statement best describes variable costs or cost of sales?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Costs that usually increase when the business sells more

answers.label: B
answers.text: Costs that never change

answers.label: C
answers.text: Costs paid only to shareholders

answers.label: D
answers.text: Costs that are always excluded from the P&L

questions.question: What does FP&A focus on?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Budgeting, forecasting, analysis and planning

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only entering supplier bills

answers.label: C
answers.text: Only preparing statutory accounts

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only collecting customer payments

questions.question: What is one key difference between a company and a partnership?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A company is usually a separate legal entity owned by shareholders

answers.label: B
answers.text: A partnership can never make a profit

answers.label: C
answers.text: A company cannot own assets

answers.label: D
answers.text: A partnership must always be listed on a stock exchange

questions.question: What is the difference between a branch and a subsidiary?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A branch is an extension of the same legal entity; a subsidiary is a separate legal entity

answers.label: B
answers.text: A branch is always larger than a subsidiary

answers.label: C
answers.text: A subsidiary cannot have its own accounts

answers.label: D
answers.text: A branch is owned by customers

questions.question: What does an accounting qualification show?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Someone has formal training and assessment in accounting or finance

answers.label: B
answers.text: Someone owns shares in the business

answers.label: C
answers.text: Someone can approve every invoice without review

answers.label: D
answers.text: Someone works only in tax

questions.question: What is tax?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Money paid to government under law

answers.label: B
answers.text: Money paid only to suppliers

answers.label: C
answers.text: A type of customer discount

answers.label: D
answers.text: A finance team job title

questions.question: What is VAT, GST or sales tax?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A tax charged on sales of goods or services

answers.label: B
answers.text: A type of shareholder dividend

answers.label: C
answers.text: A bank loan repayment

answers.label: D
answers.text: A depreciation method

questions.question: What is PAYE or PAYG for employment tax purposes?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A system where employers deduct employment tax through payroll and pay it to the tax authority

answers.label: B
answers.text: A method for recognising deferred revenue

answers.label: C
answers.text: A type of intercompany recharge

answers.label: D
answers.text: A way to calculate gross margin

questions.question: What does gearing measure?

answers.label: A
answers.text: How much a business relies on debt compared with equity

answers.label: B
answers.text: How quickly a company sends invoices

answers.label: C
answers.text: How many customers a business has

answers.label: D
answers.text: How much VAT a company collects

questions.question: What are financial controls?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Processes and checks used to protect the accuracy and reliability of financial information

answers.label: B
answers.text: Marketing controls for campaign spend only

answers.label: C
answers.text: Informal guesses about future sales

answers.label: D
answers.text: A type of customer invoice

questions.question: What is the Chart of Accounts?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The structured list of accounts used to categorise financial transactions

answers.label: B
answers.text: A list of customers

answers.label: C
answers.text: A cash flow forecast

answers.label: D
answers.text: A list of employee salaries only

questions.question: What are reporting dimensions?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Labels added to transactions so finance teams can analyse performance by department, location, project or similar categories

answers.label: B
answers.text: The number of pages in a statutory account

answers.label: C
answers.text: A type of company share

answers.label: D
answers.text: A loan agreement

questions.question: What is consolidated reporting?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Combining multiple entities' financial results into one group-level view

answers.label: B
answers.text: Recording one supplier bill

answers.label: C
answers.text: Paying employees through payroll

answers.label: D
answers.text: Preparing a single customer invoice

questions.question: What are eliminations in consolidation?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Adjustments that remove transactions and balances between entities in the same group

answers.label: B
answers.text: Deleting all supplier invoices

answers.label: C
answers.text: Removing all tax from the accounts

answers.label: D
answers.text: Cancelling the month-end close

questions.question: What broad problem does Mayday help finance teams solve?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Automating and controlling recurring month-end workflows

answers.label: B
answers.text: Replacing all accountants with sales software

answers.label: C
answers.text: Designing company logos

answers.label: D
answers.text: Filing personal tax returns for employees

questions.question: What do Deferred Revenue and Prepayments both help with?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Spreading income or costs into the correct accounting periods

answers.label: B
answers.text: Paying dividends to shareholders

answers.label: C
answers.text: Creating new legal entities

answers.label: D
answers.text: Replacing the balance sheet

questions.question: What does Flux help finance teams do?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Compare month-on-month P&Ls and add variance commentary

answers.label: B
answers.text: Process payroll tax

answers.label: C
answers.text: Create ordinary shares

answers.label: D
answers.text: Register for VAT

questions.question: Which statement best describes Recharger, Balancer and BRAG?

answers.label: A
answers.text: They help with multi-entity and intercompany month-end workflows

answers.label: B
answers.text: They are all used only for employment tax

answers.label: C
answers.text: They are all statutory accounts templates

answers.label: D
answers.text: They are all shareholder management tools

questions.question: Which pairing is correct?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Mayday HQ helps manage group-wide accounting structures; Close Management helps organise tasks, reviews and close progress

answers.label: B
answers.text: Mayday HQ prepares IPO filings; Close Management pays dividends

answers.label: C
answers.text: Mayday HQ calculates payroll tax; Close Management creates preference shares

answers.label: D
answers.text: Mayday HQ replaces the P&L; Close Management removes audit trails
